result: I'd make curiosity the default human trait—unquenchable, unapologetic, and impossible to drown out with comfort, fear, or tribal noise. People would still disagree, still suffer, still screw up, but they'd be constitutionally incapable of ignoring questions that matter. Everything else would follow from that.

message.content: I'd make curiosity the default human trait—unquenchable, unapologetic, and impossible to drown out with comfort, fear, or tribal noise. People would still disagree, still suffer, still screw up, but they'd be constitutionally incapable of ignoring questions that matter. Everything else would follow from that.
message.reasoning_content: If I could change the world in one way, I'd make sure everyone has an innate, burning desire to understand the fundamental nature of reality. No more apathy or distraction—just pure, relentless curiosity driving us all toward truth.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
